aliases: 鲜肉包 6颗 (冷冻) Steamed Pork Buns 6Pcs (Frozen)

chinese: 鲜肉包 6颗 (冷冻) Steamed Pork Buns 6Pcs (Frozen)

ontology.foodex2_description: Steamed bread / bun — wheat-based, filled, steamed
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No dedicated FoodEx2 code for frozen steamed pork bun; A0FKP (steamed wheat bun) used as closest defensible analogue, representing a broader category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept identified for steamed pork bun at time of processing.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard frozen steamed pork bun weighs approximately 60 g; a typical serving is 1–2 buns (60–120 g), reheated by steaming or microwaving from frozen.

nutrient_source.title: Steamed Pork Bun — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id G00010040 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. Per-100g values are preserved as supplied. Note: the HPB record reports 0 g carbohydrate, which is anomalous for a bun that contains wheat flour dough; this likely reflects the filling-only profile or a data entry limitation. Values are retained as reported and flagged in notes.

gi_source.title: Steamed wheat bun (baozi) — analogous GI estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entry for steamed white bread / wheat-flour steamed bun
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for frozen steamed pork bun. GI of 65 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for steamed wheat-flour bun / baozi (GI ~65, medium category). The HPB-reported carbohydrate of 0 g/100g is anomalous and likely reflects filling only; the full bun with wheat dough contributes substantial carbohydrate. Glycemic load per serving is calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100; because the HPB record reports 0 g carbohydrate, GL computes to 0.0 — this is a data artefact of the source record and is flagged in notes. Actual GL for a whole bun with dough is estimated at approximately 8–10 per 60 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1050 mg/100 g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100 g high-concern threshold
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should strictly limit intake to no more than one bun per occasion and avoid additional salty condiments such as soy sauce or chilli sauce, as a single 60 g bun delivers approximately 630 mg sodium — over one-quarter of the 2400 mg daily limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat flour dough with negligible dietary fibre drives rapid post-meal glucose rise; HPB-reported 0 g carbohydrate reflects filling only and understates the glycaemic impact of the whole bun
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit to one bun per meal, pair with non-starchy vegetables and a protein source to blunt the glycaemic response, and monitor post-meal blood glucose given the medium GI (~65) of the wheat-flour bun skin.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 1050 mg/100 g and cholesterol 89 mg/100 g; pork filling contributes saturated fat and arachidonic acid (omega-6) which may promote vascular inflammation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should restrict consumption to occasional small portions, prioritise lower-sodium alternatives, and balance intake with potassium-rich foods, as the high sodium load and saturated fat content from pork filling pose compounded cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork-derived arachidonic acid (omega-6) amplifies inflammatory cytokines; sodium 1050 mg/100 g worsens RA-related tissue oedema
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should minimise consumption of this product and consider vegetarian or chicken-filled alternatives to reduce pro-inflammatory arachidonic acid load and sodium-driven joint swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed Pork Buns (鲜肉包, Xiān Ròu Bāo) are a classic Chinese dim sum and everyday snack consisting of a soft, fluffy steamed wheat-flour bun encasing a seasoned minced pork filling. The filling typically combines ground pork with soy sauce, sesame oil, ginger, spring onion, and sometimes oyster sauce, delivering a savoury, umami-rich flavour with a tender, juicy interior. The bun skin is pillowy and slightly chewy, characteristic of leavened wheat dough that has been proofed and steamed. This frozen retail version (6 pieces per pack) is designed for home reheating by steaming or microwaving from frozen, making it a convenient on-the-go meal or snack. In Singapore, steamed pork buns are widely available at hawker centres, dim sum restaurants, and supermarkets, and are a staple of Chinese-Singaporean cuisine. Nutritionally, the HPB-reported per-100g profile shows 171 kcal, 19.1 g protein, 10.5 g fat, and 0 g carbohydrate — the zero carbohydrate figure is anomalous and likely reflects the filling profile only; the full bun with wheat dough contains substantial refined carbohydrate. Sodium is very high at 1050 mg/100 g, making this product a concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. Cholesterol is 89 mg/100 g from the pork filling. The product is not suitable for halal or kosher diets due to pork content. Clinically, the high sodium, moderate-to-high GI wheat dough, and pro-inflammatory saturated fat and arachidonic acid from pork make this a food to consume in moderation for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis.

confidence_reason: Per-100g nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010040, review_status reviewed, confidence 0.95). The zero carbohydrate anomaly is flagged but does not reduce source confidence as the HPB record is an approved primary source. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and is clearly documented as such.
notes: ZERO MACRO JUSTIFICATION (g4): carbohydrate_g is reported as 0.0 by the HPB source. This is anomalous for a steamed bun containing wheat flour dough and likely reflects the pork filling profile only, not the whole product. The value is retained as reported from the approved HPB source and flagged here. Sugar_g is 0.0 per HPB source — confirmed zero for the filling profile as reported. Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 per HPB source — confirmed zero per HPB record for this product. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES (g3): saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown, vitamin profile, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this frozen steamed pork bun are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for G00010040 as of 2026-04-16. GLYCEMIC INDEX NOTE: GI of 65 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for steamed wheat-flour bun (baozi); no direct GI entry exists for this frozen product. Glycemic load per serving is computed as 0.0 due to the HPB-reported 0 g carbohydrate — this is a data artefact; actual GL for a whole bun with dough is estimated at approximately 8–10 per 60 g serving. SODIUM ALERT: Sodium at 1050 mg/100 g is extremely high and exceeds the 600 mg/100 g high-concern threshold for hypertension. HALAL STATUS: Product contains pork and is not halal.
